Site Id.description: Landing facility site number - a unique identifying number which, together with the landing facility type code, forms the key to the airport record. (ex. 04508.*A)

Facility Type.description: Landing facility type. (ex. Airport, Balloonport, Seaplane Base, Gliderport, Heliport,  Ultralight)

Loc Id.description: Location identifier unique 3-4 character alphanumeric identifier assigned to the landing facility. (ex. 'ORD' for Chicago O'Hare)

Effective Date.description: Information effective date (mm/dd/yyyy). This date coincides with the 56-day charting and publication cycle date.

Region.description: FAA region code. (ex. AAL - Alaska, ACE - Central, AEA - Eastern, AGL - Great Lakes, AIN - International, ANE - New England, ANM - Northwest Mountain, ASO - Southern, ASW - Southwest, AWP - Western-Pacific)

ADO.description: FAA district or field office code. (ex. CHI)

State Id.description: Associated state post office code standard two letter abbreviation for U.S. states and territories. (ex. IL, PR, CQ)

County State.description: Associated county's state (post office code) state where the associated county is located; may not be the same as the associated city's state code. (ex. IL)

Name.description: Official facility name. (ex. Chicago O'Hare Intl)

Ownership.description: Airport ownership type. (ex. PU - publicly owned, PR - privately owned, MA - air force owned, MN - navy owned, MR - army owned)

Use.description: Facility use. (ex. PU - open to the public, PR - private)

Owner City,State,Zip.description: Owner's city, state and zip code.

Owner Phone.description: Owner's phone number. (data formats: nnn-nnn-nnnn (area code + phone number), 1-nnn-nnnn (dial 1-800 then number), 8-nnn-nnnn (dial 800 then number)

Manager City,State,Zip.description: Manager's city, state and zip code.

Manager Phone.description: Manager's phone number. (data formats: nnn-nnn-nnnn (area code + phone number), 1-nnn-nnnn (dial 1-800 then number), 8-nnn-nnnn (dial 800 then number)

ARP Method.description: Airport reference point determination method. (ex. E - estimated, S - surveyed)

Elevation.description: Airport elevation (nearest foot MSL). Elevation is measured at the highest point on the centerline of the usable landing surface. (ex. 1200; -10 for 10 feet below sea level)

Elevation Method.description: Airport elevation determination method. (ex. E - estimated, S - surveyed)

Magnetic Variation.description: Magnetic variation and direction magnetic variation to nearest degree. (ex. 03W)

Sectional.description: Aeronautical sectional chart on which facility appears. (ex. Washington)

Distance From CBD.description: Distance from central business district of the associated city to the airport (nearest nautical mile - ex. 08).

Direction From CBD.description: Direction of airport from central business district of associated city (nearest 1/8 compass point - ex. NE).

Land Area.description: Amount of land owned by the airport in acres.

ARTCC Id.description: Boundary ARTCC Identifier. The boundary ARTCC is the FAA air route traffic control center within whose published boundaries the airport lies. It may not be the controlling ARTCC for the airport if a letter of agreement exists between the boundary ARTCC and another ARTCC. (ex. ZDC for Washington ARTCC)

ARTCC Computer ID.description: Boundary ARTCC (FAA) computer identifier. (ex. ZCW for Washington ARTCC)

Responsible ARTCC Id.description: Responsible ARTCC identifier the responsible ARTCC is the FAA air route traffic control center who has assumed control over the airport through a letter of agreement with the boundary ARTCC. (ex. ZDC for Washington ARTCC)

Responsible ARTCC Computer Id.description: Responsible ARTCC (FAA) computer identifier. (ex. ZCW for Washington ARTCC)

Tie In FSS.description: Tie-in FSS physically located on facility. (ex. Y - tie-in FSS is on the airport, n - tie-in FSS is not on the airport)

Tie In FSS Id.description: Tie-in flight service station (FSS) identifier. (ex. DCA for Washington FSS)

FSS Phone Number.description: Local phone number from airport to FSS for administrative services

FSS Toll Free Number.description: Toll free phone number from airport to FSS for pilot briefing services the data describes the type of toll-free communications and the number to dial. The data formats and their meanings are: 1-nnn-nnnn, dial 1-800- then nnn-nnnn; 8-nnn-nnnn, dial 800 then nnn-nnnn; e-nnnnnnnn, enterprise number dial 0 & ask for enterprise nnnnnnnn; lcnnn-nnnn, local call - dial nnn-nnnn; dl, direct line telephone at the airport - no dialing required; z-nnnnnnnn, zenith number - dial 0 and ask for zenith nnnnnnnn; w-nnnnnnnn, dial 0 and ask for wx nnnnnnnn; c-nnnnnnnn, dial 0 and ask for commerce nnnnnnnn; ld-nnnnnnnn, long distance call - dial (area code) then nnnnnnn; lt-nnnnnnnn, long distal call dial 1-nnnnnnn; 1-wx-brief, dial 1-800-wx-brief; 8-wx-brief, dial 800-wx-brief

Alternate FSS Id.description: Alternate FSS identifier provides the identifier of a full-time flight service station that assumes responsibility for the airport during the off hours of a part-time primary FSS. (ex. 'DCA' for Washington FSS)

Alternate FSS Name.description: Alternate FSS name. (ex. 'Washington' for Washington FSS)

Alternate FSS Toll Free Number.description: Toll free phone number from airport to FSS for pilot briefing services the data describes the type of toll-free communications and the number to dial. The data formats and their meanings are: 1-nnn-nnnn, dial 1-800- then nnn-nnnn; 8-nnn-nnnn, dial 800 then nnn-nnnn; e-nnnnnnnn, enterprise number dial 0 & ask for enterprise nnnnnnnn; lcnnn-nnnn, local call - dial nnn-nnnn; dl, direct line telephone at the airport - no dialing required; z-nnnnnnnn, zenith number - dial 0 and ask for zenith nnnnnnnn; w-nnnnnnnn, dial 0 and ask for wx nnnnnnnn; c-nnnnnnnn, dial 0 and ask for commerce nnnnnnnn; ld-nnnnnnnn, long distance call - dial (area code) then nnnnnnn; lt-nnnnnnnn, long distal call dial 1-nnnnnnn; 1-wx-brief, dial 1-800-wx-brief; 8-wx-brief, dial 800-wx-brief.

NOTAM Facility Id.description: Identifier of the facility responsible for issuing notices to airmen (NOTAMS) and weather information for the airport. (ex. ORD)

NOTAM Service.description: Availability of NOTAM 'd' service at airport. (ex. Y - yes, N - no)

Activation Date.description: Airport activation date (mm/yyyy). Provides the month and year that the facility was added to the NFDC airport database. Note: this information is only available for those facilities opened since 1981. (ex. 06/1981)

Airport Status Code.description: Airport status code: CI - closed indefinitely; CP - closed permanently; O - operational

Certification Type Date.description: Airport certification type and date. Format is the class code ('I', 'II', 'III' or 'IV') followed by a one character code A, B, C, D, E, followed by a one character code S or U, followed by the month and year of certification. (ex. 'I A S 07/1980', 'I C S 01/1983' or 'I A U 09/1983'). Codes A, B, C, D, and E are for airports having a full certificate under CFR Part 139, and receiving scheduled air carrier service from carriers certificated by the Civil Aeronautics Board. The A, B, C, D, and E identify the aircraft rescue and firefighting index for the airport. Code L is for airports having limited certification under CFR Part 139. Code S is for Airports receiving scheduled air carrier service from carriers certificated by the Civil Aeronautics Board. Code U is for airports not receiving this scheduled service.

Federal Agreements.description: NPIAS/Federal Agreement Code. A combination of 1 to 7 codes that indicate the type of federal agreements existing at the airport. (ex. NGH). N - national plan of integrated airport systems (NPIAS); B - installation of navigational facilities on privately owned airports under F&E program; G - grant agreements under FAAP/ADAP/AIP; H - compliance with accessibility to the handicapped; P - surplus property agreement under Public Law 289; R - surplus property agreement under Regulation 16-WAA; S - conveyance under section 16, Federal Airport Act of 1946 or Section 23, Airport and Airway Development Act of 1970; V - advance planning agreement under FAAP; X - obligations assumed by transfer; Y - assurances pursuant to Title VI, Civil Rights Act of 1964; Z - conveyance under Section 303(C), Federal Aviation Act of 1958; 1 - grant agreement has expired, however, agreement remains in effect for this facility as long as it is public use. 2 - section 303(c) authority from FAA act of 1958 has expired; however, agreement remains in effect for this facility as long as it is public use. 3 - ap-4 agreement under DLAND or DCLA has expired; none - no grant agreement exists; blank- no grant agreement exists

Airspace Determination.description: Airport airspace analysis determination. (ex. CONDL (conditional), NOT ANALYZED, NO OBJECTION, OBJECTIONABLE)

Customs Airport Of Entry.description: Facility has been designated by the U.S. Treasury as an international airport of entry for customs (ex. Y - yes, N - no)

Customs Landing Rights.description: Facility has been designated by the U.S. Treasury as a customs landing rights airport (ex. Y - yes, N - no)

Military Joint Use.description: Facility has military/civil joint use agreement that allows civil operations at a military airport or military operations at a civil airport (ex. Y - yes, N - no)

Military Landing Rights.description: Airport has entered into an agreement that grants landing rights to the military (ex. Y - yes, N - no)

Inspection Method.description: Airport inspection method. (ex. F - federal, S - state, C - contractor, 1 - 5010-1 public use mail out program, 2 - 5010-2 private use mail out program)

Inspection Group.description: Agency/group performing physical inspection (ex. F - faa airports field personnel, s - state aeronautical personnel, c - private contract personnel, n - owner)

Last Owner Information Date.description: Last date information response was completed by facility owner or manager (mmddyyyy)

Fuel Types.description: Fuel types available for public use at the airport. There can be up to 8 occurrences of a fixed 5 character field (ex. 100__100LL__). 100 - Grade 100 Gasoline (green), 100LL - Grade 100 Low-Lead Gasoline (blue), A++ - Jet A, Kerosene, with FS–II*, CI/LI#, SDA##, FP** minus 40ºC., A++10 - (A++100) Jet A, Kerosene, with FS–II*, CI/LI#, SDA##, FP** minus 40ºC, with +100 fuel additive that improves thermal stability characteristics of kerosene jet fuels., A1 - Jet A–1, Kerosene, without FS–II*, FP** minus 47ºC., A1+ - Jet A–1, Kerosene with FS–II*, FP** minus 47º C., A - Jet A, Kerosene, without FS–II*, FP** minus 40º C., A+ - Jet A Kerosene with Icing Inhibitor, J - (Jet Fuel Type Unknown), J5 - (JP5) (JP–5 military specification) Kerosene with FS–II, FP** minus 46ºC., J8 - (JP8) (JP–8 military specification) Jet A–1, Kerosene with FS–II*, CI/LI#, SDA##, FP** minus 47ºC., J8+10 - (J8+100) (JP–8 military specification) Jet A–1, Kerosene with FS–II*, CI/LI#, SDA##, FP** minus 47ºC, with +100 fuel additive that improves thermal stability characteristics of kerosene jet fuels., MOGAS - Automobile gasoline which is to be used as aircraft fuel., UL91 - Unleaded Grade 91 Gasoline, UL94 - Unleaded Grade 94 Gasoline,   UL100 - Unleaded 100 Grade Gasoline

Airframe Repair.description: Airframe repair service availability/type. (ex. MAJOR, MINOR, NONE)

Power Plant Repair.description: Power plant (engine) repair availability/type. (ex. MAJOR, MINOR, NONE)

Bottled Oxygen Type.description: Type of bottled oxygen available (value represents high and/or low pressure replacement bottle). (ex. HIGH, LOW, HIGH/LOW, NONE)

Bulk Oxygen Type.description: Type of bulk oxygen available (value represents high and/or low pressure cylinders). (ex. HIGH, LOW, HIGH/LOW, NONE)

Lighting Schedule.description: Airport lighting schedule value is the beginning-ending times (local time) that lights are operated. Format can be 1900-2300, DUSK-0100, ALL, DUSK-DAWN, NONE, etc.

Beacon Schedule.description: Beacon lighting schedule value is the beginning-ending times (local time) that the rotating airport beacon light is operated. Value can be "SS-SR" (indicating sunset-sunrise), blank, or "SEE RMK", indicating that the details are in a facility remark data entry.

ATCT.description: Air traffic control tower located on airport. (ex. Y - yes, N - no)

UNICOM.description: Unicom frequencies available at the airport there can be up to 6 occurrences of a fixed 7 character field. (ex. 122.700 or 122.700122.800 or NONE)

Segmented Circle.description: Segmented circle airport marker system on the airport. (ex. Y - yes, N - no, none)

Beacon Color.description: Lens color of operable beacon located on the airport. (ex. W - white; Y-yellow; G-green; WG - white-green (civil airport); WY - white-yellow (seaplane base); WGY - white-green-yellow (heliport); SWG - split-white-green (military airport)

Non Commercial Landing Fee.description: Landing fee charged to non-commercial users of airport. (ex. Y - yes, N - no)

Medical Use.description: Landing facility is used for medical purposes. (ex. Y - yes, N - no)

Single Engine Aircraft.description: Number of single engine general aviation aircraft.

Multi Engine Aircraft.description: Number of multi engine general aviation aircraft.

Jet Engine Aircraft.description: Number of jet engine general aviation aircraft.

Commercial Operations.description: Commercial services. Scheduled operations by cab-certificated carriers or intrastate carriers.

Commuter Operations.description: Commuter services. Scheduled commuter and cargo carriers.

Air Taxi Operations.description: Air taxi. Air taxi operators carrying passengers, mail, or mail for revenue.

GA Local Operations.description: General aviation local operations. Those operating in the local traffic pattern or within a 20-mile radius of the airport.

GA Itin Operations.description: General aviation itinerant operations. Those general aviation operations (excluding commuter or air taxi) not qualifying as local.

Operations Date.description: 12-month ending date on which annual operations data in above six field is based (mm/dd/yyyy).

Fuel Available.description: Contract fuel available. (ex. Y - yes, N - no)

Transient Storage.description: Transient storage. (ex. Y - yes, N - no, none)

Other Services.description: Other services. (ex. Y - yes, N - no, none)

Wind Indicator.description: Wind direction indicator. (ex. Y - yes, N - no, none)

NPIAS Hub.description: NPIAS Hub Type (Large, Medium, Small, Non-Hub)

Site Id.description: Landing facility site number. The unique identifying number of the airport whose associated remarks are being described. Together with the remark element number field, this provides the unique key to a remark record.

Loc Id.description: Location identifier unique 3-4 character alphanumeric identifier assigned to the landing facility. (ex. 'ORD' for Chicago O'Hare)

State Id.description: Landing facility state post office code. The state where the landing facility is located. This is used in sorting the entire file by state and site number.

Remark Element Name.description: Remark element name. This information relates a remark to one of the previously described data elements. For specific remarks related to an airport or common runway data, the value will be just the data element name. For specific remarks pertaining to a runway end the format will be the data element name followed by the runway end. For non-specific remarks, this field will be A110-N where N is a sequential number (ex. E111, A110-3, A42-18, A55-H1)

Remark.description: Remark text. Free form text that further describes a specific information item or may be general in nature.

Site Id.description: Landing facility site number. The unique identifying number of the airport whose runway is being described. Together with the runway id field, this provides the unique key to a runway record.

Loc Id.description: Location identifier unique 3-4 character alphanumeric identifier assigned to the landing facility. (ex. 'ORD' for Chicago O'Hare)

State.description: Runway state post office code. The state where the landing facility is located. This was used in sorting the entire file by state and site number.

Runway Id.description: Runway identification. Ex. 01/19; 18L/36R (parallel runways); H1 (helipad); N/S (north/south); ALL/WAY (seaplane); B1 (balloonport)

Surface Type Condition.description: Runway surface type and condition. CONC - Portland cement concrete; ASPH - asphalt or bituminous concrete; SNOW - snow; ICE - ice; MATS - pierced steel planking, landing mats, membranes; TREATED - oiled, soil cement or lime stabilized; GRAVEL - gravel, cinders, crushed rock, coral or shells, slag; TURF - grass, sod; DIRT - natural soil; WATER - water; E - excellent condition; G - good condition; F - fair condition; P - poor condition; L - failed condition

Surface Treatment.description: Runway surface treatment. (ex. GRVD - saw-cut or plastic grooved; PFC - porous friction course; AFSC - aggregate friction seal coat; RFSC - rubberized friction seal coat; WC - wire comb or wire tine; NONE - no special surface treatment)

PCN.description:
Pavement classification number (pcn) 
a rating system that expresses the relative
load carrying capacity of a pavement in terms
of a standard single wheel load. the rating
is structured so that a pavement with a
particular pcn value can support, without
weight restrictions, an aircraft which has
an aircraft classification number (acn) equal
to or less than the pavement's pcn value.
(ex. 61/f/b/x/t)
the data format is pcn/pavement type/subgrade
strength/tire pressure/determination method
where:
  pcn: a numerical value
  pavement type:  r - rigid   f - flexible
  subgrade strength: letters a-f
  tire pressure code: letters w-z
  determination method: t - technical
  u - using aircraft
see faa advisory circular 150/5335-5 for
code definitions and pcn determination formula.

Edge Light Intensity.description: Runway lights edge intensity. (ex. HIGH - high; MED - medium; LOW - low; NSTD - non-standard lighting system; NONE - no edge lighting system)

WBC Single.description: Pavement strength under single wheel landing gear in thousands of pounds.

WBC Dual.description: Pavement strength under dual wheel landing gear in thousands of pounds.

WBC Two Dual.description: Pavement strength under dual tandem wheel landing gear in thousands of pounds.

WBC Tandem Two Dual.description: Pavement strength under dual dual tandem wheel landing gear in thousands of pounds.

Base End Id.description: Base end identifier. The runway end described by the following information. Relates to the first part of the runway identification. (ex. For runway 18/36 the base end is 18)

Base True Heading.description: Runway end true alignment true magnetic heading of the runway - to the nearest degree. (ex. For runway end 18, could be 184)

Base ILS Type.description:
Instrument Landing System (ILS) Type
 ILS       - Instrument Landing System
 MLS       - Microwave Landing System
 SDF       - Simplified Directional Facility
 Localizer - Localizer
 LDA       - Localizer-Type Directional Aid
 ISMLS     - Interim Standard Microwave
             Landing System
 ILS/DME   - Instrument Landing System/
             Distance Measuring Equipment
 SDF/DME   - Simplified Directional Facility
             Distance Measuring Equipment
 LOC/DME   - Localizer/
             Distance Measuring Equipment
 LOC/GS    - Localizer/Glide Slope
 LDA/DME   - Localizer-Type Directional Aid
             Distance Measuring Equipment

Base Right Hand Traffic Pattern.description: Right hand traffic pattern for landing aircraft. (ex. Y - yes; N - no)

Base Marking Type.description: Runway markings (type). (ex. PIR - precision instrument; NPI – non-precision instrument; BSC - basic; NRS - numbers only; NSTD - nonstandard (other than numbers only); BUOY - buoys (seaplane base); STOL - short takeoff and landing; NONE - none)

Base Marking Condition.description: Runway markings (condition). (ex. G - good; F - fair; P - poor)

Base Elevation.description: Elevation (feet MSL) at physical runway end. (ex. 58 120.5 13.0)

Base Threshold Height.description: Threshold crossing height (feet AGL). Height that the effective visual glide path crosses above the runway threshold. (ex. 32)

Base Visual Glide Path Angle.description: Visual glide path angle (hundredths of degrees). (ex. 3.00)

Base Displaced Threshold Length.description: Displaced threshold - length in feet from runway end. (ex. 120 or NONE)

Base VGSI.description: Visual glide slope indicators. Acronyms: NSTD - Any visual approach slope indicator system not approved by the FAA. Select NSTD when a non-standard system exists. Enter a referenced remark describing the NSTD VGSI; P2L - 2-box Precision Approach Path Indicator (PAPI) on the Left side of the runway; P2R - 2-box Precision Approach Path Indicator (PAPI) on the Right side of the runway; P4L - 4-box Precision Approach Path Indicator (PAPI) on the Left Side of the runway; P4R - 4-box Precision Approach Path Indicator (PAPI) on the Right side of the runway; PNIL - A System of Panels (APAP) used for alignment of an approach path, which may or may not be lighted, on the Left side of the runway; PNIR - A System of Panels (APAP) used for alignment of an approach path, which may or may not be lighted, on the Right Side of the runway; PSIL - Pulsating/Steady Burning Visual Approach Slope Indicator (PVASI) on the Left side of the runway, normally a single light unit projecting two colors; PSIR - Pulsating/Steady Burning Visual Approach Slope Indicator (PVASI) on the Right side of the runway, normally a single light unit projecting two colors; PVT - A Privately Owned, for Private Use Only, approach slope indicator light system installed on a public-use facility; S2L - 2-box Simplified Abbreviated Visual Approach Slope Indicator (SAVASI) on the Left side of the runway; S2R - 2-box Simplified Abbreviated Visual Approach Slope Indicator (SAVASI) on the Right side of the runway; TRIL - Tri-Color Visual Approach Slope Indicator (TRCV) on the Left side of the runway, normally a single light unit projecting three colors; TRIR - Tri-Color Visual Approach Slope Indicator (TRCV) on the Right side of the runway, normally a single light unit projecting three colors; V12 - 12-box Visual Approach Slope Indicator (VASI) on both sides of the runway; V16 - 16-box Visual Approach Slope Indicator (VASI) on both sides of the runway; V2L - 2-box Visual Approach Slope Indicator (VASI) on the Left side of the runway; V2R - 2-box Visual Approach Slope Indicator (VASI) on the Right side of the runway; V4L - 4-box Visual Approach Slope Indicator (VASI) on the Left Side of the runway; V4R - 4-box Visual Approach Slope Indicator (VASI) on the Right side of the runway; V6L - 6-box Visual Approach Slope Indicator (VASI) on the Left side of the runway; V6R - 6-box Visual Approach Slope Indicator (VASI) on the Right side of the runway; VAS - VAS-Non-Specific VASI

Base RVR.description: Runway visual range equipment (RVR). Indicates location(s) at which RVR equipment is installed. Can be any one or a combination of the following three one letter codes: T - touchdown; M - midfield; R - rollout; N - no RVR available. Possible values: T,M,R,N,TM,TR,MR,TMR

Base RVV.description: Runway visibility value equipment (RVR). Indicates presence of RVR equipment (ex. Y - yes; N - no)

Base ALS.description:
Approach light system. AFOVRN - Air Force Overrun; ALSF - 3000-Foot-High Intensity Approach System with Centerline Sequence Flashers; ALSF1 - Standard 2,400-Foot-High Intensity Approach System With Sequenced Flashers - Category I Configuration; ALSF2 - Standard 2,400-Foot High Intensity Approach System With Sequenced Flashers - Category II or III Configuration 
MALS - 1,400-Foot Medium Intensity Approach Light System; MALSF - 1,400-Foot Medium Intensity Approach Light System with Sequenced Flasher Lights; MALSR - 1,400-Foot Medium Intensity Approach Light System with Runway Alignment Indicator Lights; MIL_OVRN - Military Overrun; NSTD - All Others are Non-Standard; ODALS - Omni-Directional Approach Lighting System. Do not show REIL in addition to ODALS because the REIL are part of this system; RAIL - Runway Alignment Indicator Lights; RLLS - Runway Lead-In Light System; SALSF - Short Aprch Light-Seq Flsh; SALS - Short Aprch Light System; SSALF - Simplified Short Approach Lighting System with Runway Sequenced Flasher Lights; SSALR - Simplified Short Approach Lighting System with Runway Alignment Indicator Lights; SSALS - Simplified Short Approach Lighting System

Base REIL.description: Runway end identifier lights (REIL) availability. (ex. Y - yes; N - no)

Base Centerline Lights.description: Runway centerline lights availability. (ex. Y - yes; N - no)

Base Touchdown Lights.description: Runway end touchdown lights availability. (ex. Y - yes; N - no)

Base Obstacle Marking Lighting.description: Controlling object marked/lighted. (ex. M - marked, L - lighted, ML - marked and lighted, NONE - none)

Base Obstacle Part77.description: FAA FAR Part 77 (objects affecting navigable airspace) runway category. (ex. A(V) - utility runway with a visual approach, B(V) - other than utility runway with a visual approach, A(NP) - utility runway with a non-precision approach, C - Other than utility runway with a non-precision approach having visibility minimums greater than ¾ mile, D - Other than utility runway with a non-precision approach having visibility minimums less than or equal to ¾ mile, PIR - Precision Instrument Runway

Base Obstacle Clearance Slope.description: Controlling object clearance slope. Value, expressed as a ratio of N:1, of the clearance that is available to approaching aircraft. If the clearance slope is greater than 50:1 then 50 or 50+ will be entered.

Base Obstacle Height Above Runway.description: Controlling object height above runway height, in feet ADL, the object is above the physical runway end.

Base Obstacle Distance From Runway.description: Controlling object distance from runway end distance, in feet, from the physical runway end to the controlling object. This is measured using the extended runway centerline to a point abeam the object. (ex. 800 0 NONE)

Base Obstacle Centerline Offset.description:
Controlling object centerline offset
 distance, in feet, that the controlling object
 is located away from the extended runway centerline
 as measured horizontally on a line perpendicular
 to the extended runway centerline. also, indicates
 the direction (left or right) to the object
 from the centerline as seen by an approaching
 pilot.  (ex. 50r, 75l, none)

Base Gradient Direction.description: Runway end gradient direction (UP or DOWN).

Base Position Source.description: Runway end position source. (ex. NACO, AVN)

Base Elevation Source.description: Runway end elevation source. (ex. NACO, AVN)

Base Displaced Position Source.description: Displaced threshold position source. (ex. NACO, AVN)

Base Elevation Position Source.description: Displaced threshold elevation source. (ex. NACO, AVN)

Base TDZ Elevation Source.description: Touchdown zone elevation source. (ex. NACO, AVN)

Base TORA.description: Declared distances Take Off Run Available, in feet.

Base TODA.description: Declared distances Take Off Distance Available, in feet.

Base ASDA.description: Declared distances Actual Stop Distance Available, in feet.

Base LDA.description: Declared distances Landing Distance Available, in feet.

Base LAHSO.description: Available landing distance for land and hold short operations (LAHSO).

Base LAHSO Id.description: ID of intersecting runway defining hold short point.

Base LAHSO Desc.description: Description of entity defining hold short point if not an intersecting runway (e.g., an intersecting taxiway).

Base LAHSO Latitude DMS.description: Latitude of LAHSO hold short point (formatted).

Base LAHSO Latitude Seconds.description: Latitude of LAHSO hold short point (seconds).

Base LAHSO Longitude DMS.description: Longitude of LAHSO hold short point (formatted).

Base LAHSO Longitude Seconds.description: Longitude of LAHSO hold short point (seconds).

Base LAHSO Source.description: LAHSO hold short point geographic coordinates source.

Base LAHSO Source Date.description: Hold short point geographic coordinates source date (mm/dd/yyyy).

Reciprocal End Id.description: Reciprocal end identifier. The runway end described by the following information. Relates to the first part of the runway identification. (ex. For runway 18/36 the reciprocal end is 36)

Reciprocal True Heading.description: Runway end true alignment true magnetic heading of the runway - to the nearest degree. (ex. For runway end 36, could be 4)

Reciprocal ILS Type.description:
Instrument Landing System (ILS) Type
 ILS       - Instrument Landing System
 MLS       - Microwave Landing System
 SDF       - Simplified Directional Facility
 Localizer - Localizer
 LDA       - Localizer-Type Directional Aid
 ISMLS     - Interim Standard Microwave
             Landing System
 ILS/DME   - Instrument Landing System/
             Distance Measuring Equipment
 SDF/DME   - Simplified Directional Facility
             Distance Measuring Equipment
 LOC/DME   - Localizer/
             Distance Measuring Equipment
 LOC/GS    - Localizer/Glide Slope
 LDA/DME   - Localizer-Type Directional Aid
             Distance Measuring Equipment

Reciprocal Right Hand Traffic Pattern.description: Right hand traffic pattern for landing aircraft. (ex. Y - yes; N - no)

Reciprocal Marking Type.description: Runway markings (type). (ex. PIR - precision instrument; NPI – non-precision instrument; BSC - basic; NRS - numbers only; NSTD - nonstandard (other than numbers only); BUOY - buoys (seaplane base); STOL - short takeoff and landing; NONE - none)

Reciprocal Marking Condition.description: Runway markings (condition). (ex. G - good; F - fair; P - poor)

Reciprocal Elevation.description: Elevation (feet MSL) at physical runway end. (ex. 58 120.5 13.0)

Reciprocal Threshold Height.description: Threshold crossing height (feet AGL). Height that the effective visual glide path crosses above the runway threshold. (ex. 32)

Reciprocal Visual Glide Path Angle.description: Visual glide path angle (hundredths of degrees). (ex. 3.00)

Reciprocal Displaced Threshold Length.description: Displaced threshold - length in feet from runway end. (ex. 120 or NONE)

Reciprocal VGSI.description: Visual glide slope indicators. Acronyms: NSTD - Any visual approach slope indicator system not approved by the FAA. Select NSTD when a non-standard system exists. Enter a referenced remark describing the NSTD VGSI; P2L - 2-box Precision Approach Path Indicator (PAPI) on the Left side of the runway; P2R - 2-box Precision Approach Path Indicator (PAPI) on the Right side of the runway; P4L - 4-box Precision Approach Path Indicator (PAPI) on the Left Side of the runway; P4R - 4-box Precision Approach Path Indicator (PAPI) on the Right side of the runway; PNIL - A System of Panels (APAP) used for alignment of an approach path, which may or may not be lighted, on the Left side of the runway; PNIR - A System of Panels (APAP) used for alignment of an approach path, which may or may not be lighted, on the Right Side of the runway; PSIL - Pulsating/Steady Burning Visual Approach Slope Indicator (PVASI) on the Left side of the runway, normally a single light unit projecting two colors; PSIR - Pulsating/Steady Burning Visual Approach Slope Indicator (PVASI) on the Right side of the runway, normally a single light unit projecting two colors; PVT - A Privately Owned, for Private Use Only, approach slope indicator light system installed on a public-use facility; S2L - 2-box Simplified Abbreviated Visual Approach Slope Indicator (SAVASI) on the Left side of the runway; S2R - 2-box Simplified Abbreviated Visual Approach Slope Indicator (SAVASI) on the Right side of the runway; TRIL - Tri-Color Visual Approach Slope Indicator (TRCV) on the Left side of the runway, normally a single light unit projecting three colors; TRIR - Tri-Color Visual Approach Slope Indicator (TRCV) on the Right side of the runway, normally a single light unit projecting three colors; V12 - 12-box Visual Approach Slope Indicator (VASI) on both sides of the runway; V16 - 16-box Visual Approach Slope Indicator (VASI) on both sides of the runway; V2L - 2-box Visual Approach Slope Indicator (VASI) on the Left side of the runway; V2R - 2-box Visual Approach Slope Indicator (VASI) on the Right side of the runway; V4L - 4-box Visual Approach Slope Indicator (VASI) on the Left Side of the runway; V4R - 4-box Visual Approach Slope Indicator (VASI) on the Right side of the runway; V6L - 6-box Visual Approach Slope Indicator (VASI) on the Left side of the runway; V6R - 6-box Visual Approach Slope Indicator (VASI) on the Right side of the runway; VAS - VAS-Non-Specific VASI

Reciprocal RVR.description: Runway visual range equipment (RVR). Indicates location(s) at which RVR equipment is installed. Can be any one or a combination of the following three one letter codes: T - touchdown; M - midfield; R - rollout; N - no RVR available. Possible values: T,M,RT,M,R,N,TM,TR,MR,TMR

Reciprocal RVV.description: Runway visibility value equipment (RVR). Indicates presence of RVR equipment (ex. Y - yes; N - no)

Reciprocal ALS.description:
Approach light system. AFOVRN - Air Force Overrun; ALSF - 3000-Foot-High Intensity Approach System with Centerline Sequence Flashers; ALSF1 - Standard 2,400-Foot-High Intensity Approach System With Sequenced Flashers - Category I Configuration; ALSF2 - Standard 2,400-Foot High Intensity Approach System With Sequenced Flashers - Category II or III Configuration 
MALS - 1,400-Foot Medium Intensity Approach Light System; MALSF - 1,400-Foot Medium Intensity Approach Light System with Sequenced Flasher Lights; MALSR - 1,400-Foot Medium Intensity Approach Light System with Runway Alignment Indicator Lights; MIL_OVRN - Military Overrun; NSTD - All Others are Non-Standard; ODALS - Omni-Directional Approach Lighting System. Do not show REIL in addition to ODALS because the REIL are part of this system; RAIL - Runway Alignment Indicator Lights; RLLS - Runway Lead-In Light System; SALSF - Short Aprch Light-Seq Flsh; SALS - Short Aprch Light System; SSALF - Simplified Short Approach Lighting System with Runway Sequenced Flasher Lights; SSALR - Simplified Short Approach Lighting System with Runway Alignment Indicator Lights; SSALS - Simplified Short Approach Lighting System

Reciprocal REIL.description: Runway end identifier lights (REIL) availability. (ex. Y - yes; N - no)

Reciprocal Centerline Lights.description: Runway centerline lights availability. (ex. Y - yes; N - no)

Reciprocal Touchdown Lights.description: Runway end touchdown lights availability. (ex. Y - yes; N - no)

Reciprocal Obstacle Marking Lighting.description: Controlling object marked/lighted. (ex. M - marked, L - lighted, ML - marked and lighted, NONE - none)

Reciprocal Obstacle Part77.description: FAA FAR Part 77 (objects affecting navigable airspace) runway category. (ex. A(V) - utility runway with a visual approach, B(V) - other than utility runway with a visual approach, A(NP) - utility runway with a non-precision approach, C - Other than utility runway with a non-precision approach having visibility minimums greater than ¾ mile, D - Other than utility runway with a non-precision approach having visibility minimums less than or equal to ¾ mile, PIR - Precision Instrument Runway

Reciprocal Obstacle Clearance Slope.description: Controlling object clearance slope. Value, expressed as a ratio of N:1, of the clearance that is available to approaching aircraft. If the clearance slope is greater than 50:1 then 50 or 50+ will be entered.

Reciprocal Obstacle Height Above Runway.description: Controlling object height above runway height, in feet ADL, the object is above the physical runway end.

Reciprocal Obstacle Distance From Runway.description: Controlling object distance from runway end distance, in feet, from the physical runway end to the controlling object. This is measured using the extended runway centerline to a point abeam the object. (ex. 800 0 NONE)

Reciprocal Obstacle Centerline Offset.description:
Controlling object centerline offset
 distance, in feet, that the controlling object
 is located away from the extended runway centerline
 as measured horizontally on a line perpendicular
 to the extended runway centerline. also, indicates
 the direction (left or right) to the object
 from the centerline as seen by an approaching
 pilot.  (ex. 50r, 75l, none)

Reciprocal Gradient Direction.description: Runway end gradient direction (UP or DOWN).

Reciprocal Position Source.description: Runway end position source. (ex. NACO, AVN)

Reciprocal Elevation Source.description: Runway end elevation source. (ex. NACO, AVN)

Reciprocal Displaced Position Source.description: Displaced threshold position source. (ex. NACO, AVN)

Reciprocal Elevation Position Source.description: Displaced threshold elevation source. (ex. NACO, AVN)

Reciprocal TDZ Elevation Source.description: Touchdown zone elevation source. (ex. NACO, AVN)

Reciprocal TORA.description: Declared distances Take Off Run Available, in feet.

Reciprocal TODA.description: Declared distances Take Off Distance Available, in feet.

Reciprocal ASDA.description: Declared distances Actual Stop Distance Available, in feet.

Reciprocal LDA.description: Declared distances Landing Distance Available, in feet.

Reciprocal LAHSO.description: Available landing distance for land and hold short operations (LAHSO).

Reciprocal LAHSO Id.description: ID of intersecting runway defining hold short point.

Reciprocal LAHSO Desc.description: Description of entity defining hold short point if not an intersecting runway (e.g., an intersecting taxiway).

Reciprocal LAHSO Latitude DMS.description: Latitude of LAHSO hold short point (formatted).

Reciprocal LAHSO Latitude Seconds.description: Latitude of LAHSO hold short point (seconds).

Reciprocal LAHSO Longitude DMS.description: Longitude of LAHSO hold short point (formatted).

Reciprocal LAHSO Longitude Seconds.description: Longitude of LAHSO hold short point (seconds).

Reciprocal LAHSO Source.description: LAHSO hold short point geographic coordinates source.

Reciprocal LAHSO Source Date.description: Hold short point geographic coordinates source date (mm/dd/yyyy).

Site Id.description: Landing facility site number - a unique identifying number which, together with the landing facility type code, forms the key to the airport record. (ex. 04508.*A)

Loc Id.description: Location identifier unique 3-4 character alphanumeric identifier assigned to the landing facility. (ex. 'ORD' for Chicago O'Hare)

State Id.description: Associated state post office code standard two letter abbreviation for U.S. states and territories. (ex. IL, PR, CQ)

Sequence.description: Attendance schedule sequence number. A number which, together with the site number, uniquely identifies the attendance schedule component.

Schedule.description: Airport attendance schedule (when minimum services are available). Format is months/days/hours where the first part describes the months that the facility is attended, then the days of the week that the facility is open, and finally the hours within the day that it is attended. This field may also contain 'UNATNDD' for unattended facilities. If there are different schedules for different times of the year or different days of the week there will be separate records for each set of months, days, and hours of attendance. (ex. 'ALL/MON-FRI/SR-SS' means the facility is open year-round, Monday thru Friday, from sunrise to sunset).
